question: Return a table of order-size buckets, with columns unit_bucket, orders, avg_order_net_value, and total_net_revenue.
sql_query:
WITH order_totals AS (
  SELECT
    oi.order_id,
    SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity,
    SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity - COALESCE(oi.discount_amount, 0)) AS order_net_value
  FROM core.orders AS o
  JOIN core.order_items AS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
  GROUP BY oi.order_id
),
buckets AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN total_quantity = 1 THEN '1'
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN '2-3'
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 4 AND 5 THEN '4-5'
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 6 AND 10 THEN '6-10'
      ELSE '11+'
    END AS unit_bucket,
    COUNT(*) AS orders,
    AVG(order_net_value) AS avg_order_net_value,
    SUM(order_net_value) AS total_net_revenue,
    -- sort key for logical ordering
    CASE
      WHEN total_quantity = 1 THEN 1
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN 2
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 4 AND 5 THEN 3
      WHEN total_quantity BETWEEN 6 AND 10 THEN 4
      ELSE 5
    END AS bucket_order
  FROM order_totals
  GROUP BY unit_bucket, bucket_order
)
SELECT unit_bucket, orders, avg_order_net_value, total_net_revenue
FROM buckets
ORDER BY bucket_order
pandas_sql_query: WITH order_units AS (SELECT o.order_id, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_units, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price - oi.discount_amount) AS order_net_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi USING (order_id) GROUP BY 1) SELECT CASE WHEN total_units = 1 THEN '1' WHEN total_units BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN '2-3' WHEN total_units BETWEEN 4 AND 6 THEN '4-6' WHEN total_units BETWEEN 7 AND 10 THEN '7-10' ELSE '11+' END AS unit_bucket, COUNT(*) AS orders, ROUND(AVG(order_net_value), 2) AS avg_order_net_value, ROUND(SUM(order_net_value), 2) AS total_net_revenue FROM order_units GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY CASE unit_bucket WHEN '1' THEN 1 WHEN '2-3' THEN 2 WHEN '4-6' THEN 3 WHEN '7-10' THEN 4 ELSE 5 END
